quest-unsent-letter-handler-ambush-loot-: You gather a travel-stained caravan badge and a torn letter page from the fray.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-clerk-fight-: Garron's smile vanishes. Ink gives way to steel as hirelings step from the ledger-shadows.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-clerk-letter: Garron shoves Adrian's sealed letter into your hands and bolts for the alley-mouth.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-lina-evidenc: You lay the caravan badge and torn page before Lina; her breath catches at the wax smear.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-lina-father-: Lina's voice thins to a wire, yet she does not look away from you.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-mara-truth-0: You press Mara gently until the story loosens—names, dates, silences.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-miner-ambush: Hoarse whistling answers you from the rocks—grey cloaks peel out of the scree.
quest-unsent-letter-handler-puzzle-q1-00: Mara draws a breath. First riddle: which road toward the quarry city drank most of the grey cloaks' fear? (Name Montor or the road there.)
quest-unsent-letter-handler-puzzle-q2-00: Second riddle: which season passed without bringing Adrian home? (Think of cold thresholds.)
quest-unsent-letter-handler-puzzle-q3-00: Third riddle: was it pride that chased him—or the silence no one would break?
quest-unsent-letter-handler-thomas-famil: Thomas exhales through his teeth, as if discipline were the only armor left.
quest-unsent-letter-fullletter-text-001:
To Mara, Lina, and Thomas,

If this reaches you, I am not coming home the way I left. The caravan's reckoning found us on the road toward Montor—grey cloaks, hired steel, names struck from ledgers as easily as chalk. I held my tongue too long, thinking silence would spare your sleep. It did not; it only fed the house's hunger for guessing.

Garron's ink knew my face before I did. What his seal covers is the truth I owe you—not neat, not gentle, but mine. Choose how loudly Britain should hear it; I will not ask you to swallow lies for the sake of my pride.

—Adrian
quest-unsent-letter-meta-completion-001: Mara brushes dust from the letter as if it were skin. "Whatever you counseled—public, private, or quiet—you gave shape to our ache. That is not nothing."
quest-unsent-letter-meta-description-001: Mara, a tailor in Britain, asks you to learn whether her husband Adrian still lives. Quarrel drives her children; caravans vanish toward Montor; a clerk in Renika hides names behind ink. Listen at hearth and ledger, then help the family choose how loudly truth should sound.
quest-unsent-letter-meta-inprogress-001: Britain listens behind polite doors. Montor's road answers in dust and blades. Renika's ledger never forgets a face. Let your quest log mark each thread—use the context menu Talk on Mara, Lina, Thomas, Henrick, or Garron when you need their dialogue window.
quest-unsent-letter-meta-refusal-001: Mara looks past your shoulder, jaw tight. "Then we keep guessing—and guessing eats the young first."
quest-unsent-letter-msg-toofar-001: You are too far away to speak with them.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-ambush-001: Walk the broken ground toward Montor until you find Henrick, a miner who flinches at his own shadow. Offer a guarded escort from his context menu Talk to lure grey-cloak ambushers into the open.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-clerk-001: In Renika, find Garron among the ledger-halls. Press him for the caravan records through Talk—expect ink to answer in steel.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-ending-001: Carry Adrian's sealed letter home: use Talk twice among Mara (Britain), Lina (Britain), and Thomas (Renika) about what it demands of the family.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-ending-002: Finally counsel Mara through Talk: choose public reckoning, private peace, or quiet watch when the dialogue offers those paths.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-evidence-0: Keep the caravan badge and torn letter page safe in your pack, then speak with Lina in Britain and show her the proof through Talk.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-puzzle-001: Return to Mara in Britain. Use Talk to begin the three road riddles at once, then answer using the dialogue choices.
quest-unsent-letter-objective-speak-fami: Hear three voices: Mara at her tailor's bench in Britain, Lina near the family hearth in Britain, and Thomas drilling in Renika. Ask after Adrian until each has answered.
quest-unsent-letter-offer-footer-001: Accept to open your quest log: each stage names where to go and what to ask. Open the NPC context menu and choose Talk on Mara, Lina, Thomas, Henrick, or Garron to continue in this window.
quest-unsent-letter-offer-pitch-001: Mara steadies a pin between her fingers until the metal warms. "My husband Adrian walked out with a caravan and never walked back. My children tear the house in two with guesses; the neighbors whisper behind bolted shutters. I need someone to listen—to them, to the road, to the silence. Will you carry this for me?"
quest-unsent-letter-reward-gold-caption-: Five hundred gold for the long road home
quest-unsent-letter-reward-memento-priva: Blessed body sash (middle torso), rare dye: +3 Dexterity
quest-unsent-letter-reward-memento-publi: Blessed body sash (middle torso), rare dye: +3 Strength
quest-unsent-letter-reward-memento-quiet: Blessed body sash (middle torso), rare dye: +3 Intelligence
quest-unsent-letter-reward-memento-previ: Blessed counsel sash for the middle torso—+3 Strength, Dexterity, or Intelligence matching your advice (included when you accept)
quest-unsent-letter-reward-thanks-001: A family's spoken thanks, a blessed counsel sash, and five hundred gold for the road.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-fallback-mara-no: Mara smooths linen without meeting your eyes. "I have no errand for you today—perhaps the road will bring another tailor's worry tomorrow."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-fallback-claim-t: Your bargain with this errand is not closed here—Mara in Britain holds the last word and the purse-strings. Seek her bench first.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-fallback-noquest: They have nothing to say to you about that.
Lina measures you briefly, then returns to her work. This is not your conversation—not yet.
Henrick squints past you toward the scree. "If you aren't here for the road's trouble, you are only clutter."
Thomas taps a buckle and keeps his eyes on drill distance. "Strangers are for market squares, not family matters."
The clerk marks a column and moves on. You have no claim on his time.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-clerk-body-001: Garron slides a ledger shut until the boards creak. "Renika keeps its debts in ink, traveler. If you lack a seal and a sponsor, you lack a reason to be in my stacks."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-clerk-hint-done-: Garron adjusts his sleeves. "The ink has had its say. Take what truth you earned and leave the stacks to their next debt."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-clerk-hint-wrong: Garron barely looks up. "My ledgers are not Britain's hearth—return when your business matches the chapter you're truly in."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-clerk-opt-001: Insist on the caravan records—Adrian's name was filed somewhere.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-ending-body: Home feels smaller with the letter in play. Lina watches the wax seal as if it might crawl. "What word do you bring back from the road?"
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-ending-opt-: Tell her plainly what Adrian's letter demands of the family.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-evidence-bo: "Evidence?" Lina's jaw tightens until a small muscle jumps. "If the stones truly spoke, I will look at what they said. Empty hands do not comfort me."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-evidence-op: Lay the caravan badge and torn page before her.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-family-body: Lina folds her arms until the knuckles pale. "If you are here about Father, do not wrap it in poetry. I have heard enough half-truths to last a life."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-family-opt-: Ask gently what she remembers of her father.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-lina-hint-wrongp: Lina shakes her head. "Not now. There are steps before this one—let your errand catch up to your boots."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-ending-body: The sealed letter feels heavier than wax. Mara searches your face. "The children read every rumor twice. Tell us what the road taught you—then help us choose how loudly home should speak of it."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-ending-opt-: Share what Adrian's letter truly says.
Urge the family to bind the truth behind their own door.
Counsel a public reckoning—let Britain hear it.
Advise quiet watchfulness—fewer words, longer memory.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-family-body: Mara leans both palms on the counter. The shop smells of wool and worry. "Adrian" is a word no one finishes aloud—yet silence cuts deeper. How do you draw her out?
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-family-opt-: Speak Adrian's name softly, then wait in quiet.
Gently insist on the whole truth.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-hint-ambush: Mara ties off a thread with more force than the cloth deserves. "Britain cannot answer what the Montor road knows. Find Henrick where the stone splits—and walk with your eyes open."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-hint-clerk-: Mara's voice lowers. "Ink in Renika remembers faces Garron would rather forget. When you are ready for that quarrel, seek him among the ledger-halls—not at my counter."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-hint-eviden: Mara taps the counter once. "Whatever those blades left in your pack must cross Lina's hands before the house can speak with one breath. Do that first."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-hint-generi: Mara exhales. "There is a proper order to sorrow. Follow the road your journal marks—and return when that page turns."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-done: Mara nods once. "That is enough for now."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-fini: Mara's voice softens. "Then we know enough to mourn without lying. Thank you for listening when it hurt."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-intr: "Travelers' gossip is a frayed rope," Mara murmurs. "Answer three small riddles of the road, and you will see how caravan dust, season, and a man's pride braid together. Ready to begin?"
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-null: Mara exhales. "Then the shape of it is plain enough. Steel your heart before you walk the Montor road."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-q1-b: First riddle: grey cloaks do not haunt every gate. Which road—think of the old quarry city—drank most of the caravan's fear?
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-q2-b: Second riddle: seasons turn whether husbands return or not. Which one slipped by with empty hands for this house?
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-q2-o: Winter—snow on the threshold, no footsteps.
Summer—heat and festivals, easy to forget.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-q3-b: Third riddle: some men flee a rival's blade; others flee a look no one will give them. What chased Adrian hardest?
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-mara-puzzle-q3-o: Silence—what the house would not say aloud.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-miner-body-001: Henrick flinches at stray gravel skittering down the scree. "If you mean to walk beside me, mean it with steel drawn—grey cloaks listen for soft promises."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-miner-hint-comba: Henrick ducks a chip of stone. "Eyes on the cloaks—talk later, if we live!"
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-miner-hint-done-: Henrick wipes grit from his palms. "You've tugged the knot loose. Britain's smoke is that way—carry what you found to the hearths that need it."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-miner-hint-wrong: Henrick jerks his chin toward the distant trail. "This quarry talk isn't for you yet—or anymore. Walk your errands in order."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-miner-opt-001: Offer a guarded escort along the Montor road.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-thomas-ending-bo: He taps the seal-wax scar on his thumb. "Paper can lie as sweetly as people. Does this letter change what we must do—or only give the wound a longer name?"
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-thomas-ending-op: Answer honestly what Adrian's letter asks of him.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-thomas-family-bo: Thomas keeps his voice low, shoulders squared like a recruit at inspection. "Family business stays family—unless you mean to carry more than gossip across our threshold."
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-thomas-family-op: Speak of family duty and Adrian's shadow.
quest-unsent-letter-rpg-thomas-hint-wron: Thomas gives a short shrug. "Wrong season for this talk. See to the threads that still tie themselves up in Britain."
quest-unsent-letter-ui-accept-reward-001: Thank them and accept the reward.
